title: Rule-Based Query Derivation Engine Pattern
summary: A lightweight, rule-based natural language task → search query derivation engine. Uses trigger term tuples mapped to query strings, with a synonym dictionary for Chinese/English term normalization. When no triggers match, falls back to configurable defaults. Supports multi-domain routing: different rule sets for UI, workflow, automation, backend, and asset queries, each independently derived from the same task text. The engine is pure string matching (no ML dependency), making it fast, deterministic, and easy to debug.
why_reusable: Any search or routing system that needs to derive structured queries from free-text task descriptions can use this pattern. It's particularly useful for AI agent tools, RAG systems, internal search engines, and CLI tools that accept natural language input.
adapter_target: Standalone QueryDerivationEngine class — takes a rule set and task text, returns derived queries. Can be dropped into any Python project needing NL→query routing.
implementation_notes: Rule format: list of (tuple_of_trigger_terms, query_string) pairs
Trigger matching is case-insensitive substring match on normalized task text
Synonym dictionary maps Chinese terms to English query terms before rule matching
Deduplication preserves insertion order; output is capped (default 5 queries)
Each domain has independent rules and defaults — domain routing is based on task keyword presence
Query normalization: strip → lowercase → synonym lookup → return
tests_required: Unit test: single trigger match returns expected query
Unit test: multiple triggers produce multiple queries without duplicates
Unit test: synonym normalization before rule matching
Unit test: defaults applied when no triggers match
risks: Hand-maintained trigger lists drift over time and miss new terminology
Synonym dictionary must be kept in sync with both Chinese and English vocabularies
Case-insensitive substring matching can produce false positives on short terms
Not suitable for ambiguous queries that need semantic understanding
do_not_do: Do not use for tasks requiring semantic similarity — this is keyword-based, not embedding-based
Do not let trigger lists grow unbounded — they become unmaintainable
Do not skip synonym normalization — Chinese/English mismatches are the most common failure mode
notes: Extracted from DataBase backend_api/app/main.py derive_queries() and related functions. The engine is ~40 lines of pure Python with no dependencies.
